title: Load abnormality detecting circuit for inverter and inverter apparatus
abstract: A load abnormality detecting circuit for an inverter to detect abnormality of a load during an operation of the inverter which has a switching element and a phase synchronizing loop controlling an output frequency to be a resonance frequency of the load, the load abnormality detecting circuit includes a phase shift detection part that detects a phase shift between an output voltage and an output current which are applied from the inverter to the load and sends an abnormal load signal based on the detected phase shift. The switching element including a self-arc-extinguishing element and a reflux diode connected in reversely parallel to the self-arc-extinguishing element. The phase shift detection part detects advance and delay of a phase of the output current with respect to the output voltage.
claims: Claims ( 6 ) A load abnormality detecting circuit for an inverter to detect abnormality of a load during an operation of the inverter which has a switching element and a phase synchronizing loop controlling an output frequency to be a resonance frequency of the load, the load abnormality detecting circuit comprising: a phase shift detection part that detects a phase shift between an output voltage and an output current which are applied from the inverter to the load and sends an abnormal load signal based on the detected phase shift,      wherein the switching element including a self-arc-extinguishing element and a reflux diode connected in reversely parallel to the self-arc-extinguishing element, and      wherein the phase shift detection part detects advance and delay of a phase of the output current with respect to the output voltage. The load abnormality detecting circuit for the inverter according to claim 1, wherein      an ON resistance value of the self-arc-extinguishing element is smaller than a forward resistance value of the reflux diode. The load abnormality detecting circuit for the inverter according to claim 1 or 2, wherein      the phase shift detection part includes a D flip-flop having a clock port to which a pulse signal based on the output current is input as a clock signal, a data port to which a pulse signal based on the output voltage is input as a data signal, and an output port outputs signals according to a state; and      a pulse width change part that sets a pulse width of the pulse signal based on the output voltage, which is input to the data port, to a pulse width shorter than a half cycle of the output voltage,      wherein the D flip-flop is transitioned from a reset state to a set state when the data signal is input at a timing defined by the clock signal. The load abnormality detecting circuit for the inverter according to claim 3, wherein      the D flip-flop includes a reset port to which a reset signal which transitions from the set state to the reset state is input, and      the load abnormality detecting circuit further comprising:      a mask part that compares a current value of the output current applied to the load with a predetermined reference value and inputs the reset signal to the reset port of the D flip-flop until the current value is larger than the reference value. An inverter apparatus which has a switching element and a phase synchronizing loop controlling an output frequency to be a resonance frequency of a load, the inverter apparatus comprising:      the load abnormality detecting circuit for the inverter according to claim 1. The inverter apparatus according to claim 5, wherein an ON resistance value of the self-arc-extinguishing element is smaller than a forward resistance value of the reflux diode.
